stem: Modern CPUs include several levels of cache memory.
model_answer: Cache is very fast memory inside (or very close to) the CPU that stores copies of instructions and data that are likely to be reused soon. Because the CPU can access cache much more quickly than main memory, fewer slow memory reads are needed and overall performance improves.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Explain how cache memory improves CPU performance.

mark_points.text: cache stores copies of frequently or recently used instructions and data
accepted_alternatives: it keeps data likely to be needed again

mark_points.text: the CPU can access cache much faster than main memory (RAM)
so fewer slow fetches from main memory are needed, and the CPU spends less time waiting
accepted_alternatives: this speeds up the fetch–decode–execute cycle
